label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's break this down step by step. First, I need to track the movements as described in the instructions. The question is whether following these steps will bring me back to the starting point. Starting at the origin point, facing a certain direction (let's say north for simplicity). 1. Take 7 steps forward. So I'm now 7 units north of the starting point. 2. Turn around. Now I'm facing south. 3. Turn around again. Turning around means 180 degrees, so now I'm back facing north. 4. Turn around again. Now facing south. 5. Take 1 step forward. Since I'm facing south, this step takes me 1 unit south, so overall position is 7 - 1 = 6 units north of start. 6. Take 6 steps forward. Still facing south, so each step moves me south. 6 units south from current position: 6 - 6 = 0. So I end up back at the starting point. Wait, let me double-check that. Starting at 0. Take 7 steps north: +7. Then turn around (south), turn around again (north), turn around again (south). Then take 1 step south: +7 -1 = +6. Then take 6
